message:
fcp-conformance: pin manifest invariants to fixture corpus so tests stop passing vacuously

[CRITICAL] `connector_manifest_differential.rs` landed with MIN_PARSE_PASSES=0
against the live `connectors/*/manifest.toml` corpus. Since the corpus
currently parses at 0 due to known drift (`[format]` vs `[manifest]`,
operation capabilities not in `capabilities.required`), every invariant
test above — uniqueness, namespace, zones, operations, id-segment — was
iterating an empty `passes` map and passing vacuously. A regression in
the invariant logic itself would not have fired.

Fix: add two fixture-sourced tests that pin the invariants to the 69
`tests/vectors/manifest/*_good.toml` canonical vectors, loaded via
`parse_str_unchecked` (fixtures ship with a placeholder interface hash,
so full `parse_str` is the wrong layer — the invariants here are
structural and live at the unchecked parse).

- `fixture_corpus_all_parses_unchecked`: regression gate with a stable
  floor (MIN_FIXTURES=60). Panics if any `_good.toml` fixture stops
  parsing, which either means the fixture broke or the parser regressed.
- `fixture_corpus_satisfies_all_invariants`: runs all 7 invariants
  (non-empty id, `fcp.` namespace, unique ids, non-empty version, home
  zone non-empty + not forbidden, >=1 operation, well-formed id segment)
  against the fixture set and reports every violation in one shot.

Leaves the live-corpus report tests alone as the drift-discovery
mechanism they were designed to be.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
